title: FDMJ Compiler (Frontend + LLVM IR Backend)
summary: A compiler for FDMJ (a Mini Java variant) that translates source through a Lex/Yacc frontend into LLVM IR via Tiger IR canonicalization, LLVM instruction selection, basic block graph construction, and SSA conversion, with a parallel analysis chain (control flow → liveness → interference graph) for optional register-allocation debugging. Seven modules form two independent implementation chains.
instructionPreview: This is the FDMJ compiler project for an undergraduate compilers course. The compiler translates FDMJ (a Mini-Java style language) source code through a frontend (lexer, parser, semantic analysis, IR translation) and an LLVM backend (instruction selection, basic block graph, SSA conversion, assembly emission). The project is built with CMake/Ninja and uses flex/bison for the frontend.
instruction:
This is the FDMJ compiler project for an undergraduate compilers course. The compiler translates FDMJ (a Mini-Java style language) source code through a frontend (lexer, parser, semantic analysis, IR translation) and an LLVM backend (instruction selection, basic block graph, SSA conversion, assembly emission). The project is built with CMake/Ninja and uses flex/bison for the frontend.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project is already present but several core subsystems have been removed and replaced with `/* TODO: implement */` stubs. You must restore their implementations so that the compiler can compile and execute FDMJ programs correctly. Build the project with `make build` (or `make rebuild` for a clean build).

## Modules to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **LLVM Instruction Selection** (`lib/backend/llvm/llvmgen.c`): Translates Tree IR statements and expressions into LLVM IR assembly instructions. Handles binary operations, memory access, conditional and unconditional jumps, move statements, return statements, method calls (both internal and external runtime calls like putint, putch, getint, malloc, putarray, getarray, starttime, stoptime), function prologues, epilogues, and body entry blocks. Uses the maximal munch approach with the AS_instr/emit infrastructure already in place. (~13 functions to implement)

- **IR Canonicalization** (`lib/optimizer/canon.c`): Transforms the Tree IR to remove SEQ and ESEQ nodes, partitions the linearized statements into basic blocks, and reorders blocks via trace scheduling so that every CJUMP is immediately followed by its false-branch label. Includes statement and expression canonicalization, basic block formation, and trace scheduling. (~8 functions to implement)

- **Control Flow Graph Construction** (`lib/optimizer/flowgraph.c`): Builds a directed graph from an assembly instruction list where each instruction is a node. Fall-through edges connect sequential instructions; branch edges connect jump/conditional-jump instructions to their target labels. Tracks use/def sets and identifies move instructions. (~1 function to implement)

- **Liveness Analysis** (`lib/optimizer/liveness.c`): Iterative dataflow analysis over the control flow graph computing live-in and live-out temporary sets for each instruction, using the standard equations: in[n] = use[n] union (out[n] - def[n]), out[n] = union of in[s] for all successors s. (~2 functions to implement)

- **Interference Graph Construction** (`lib/optimizer/ig.c`): Builds the interference graph for register allocation. For each instruction, creates interference edges between defined temporaries and live-out temporaries (with special handling for move instructions to avoid spurious interference between source and destination). (~2 functions to implement)

- **Basic Block Graph** (`lib/optimizer/bg.c`): Constructs the basic block graph from an assembly block list, registering blocks as nodes and adding edges according to successor labels, then removing unreachable blocks (dead code elimination). (~1 function to implement)

- **SSA Conversion** (`lib/backend/llvm/ssa.c`): Converts the LLVM IR from the basic block graph into SSA form. Includes topological sorting of the CFG, iterative liveness analysis at the block level, dominator set computation, immediate dominator derivation, dominance frontier calculation, phi-function insertion, and variable renaming via DFS over the dominator tree. (~10 functions to implement)

moduleDag.description: A compiler for FDMJ (a Mini Java variant) that translates source through a Lex/Yacc frontend into LLVM IR via Tiger IR canonicalization, LLVM instruction selection, basic block graph construction, and SSA conversion, with a parallel analysis chain (control flow → liveness → interference graph) for optional register-allocation debugging. Seven modules form two independent implementation chains.

nodes.label: IR Canonicalization
nodes.description: Transforms Tiger IR trees into canonical form by removing SEQ/ESEQ nodes (linearize), partitioning the linearized statement list into well-formed basic blocks (LABEL-started, JUMP/CJUMP/RETURN-ended), and reordering blocks via trace scheduling so that every CJUMP is immediately followed by its false-branch label. Produces the C_stmListList consumed by LLVM instruction selection.

nodes.label: Control Flow Graph Construction
nodes.description: Builds a per-instruction directed control flow graph from a flat assembly instruction list. Adds fall-through edges between sequential instructions and branch edges from jump/conditional-jump instructions to their labeled targets. Populates a use-def (UD) table recording use/def temporary sets and move status for each node; these are queried by liveness and interference graph construction.

nodes.label: LLVM Instruction Selection
nodes.description: Translates canonicalized Tiger IR basic blocks into LLVM IR assembly instructions using maximal munch. Handles all statement kinds (LABEL, JUMP, CJUMP, MOVE, EXP, RETURN) and all expression kinds (BINOP, MEM, TEMP, NAME, CONST, CALL, ExtCALL), including external runtime calls (malloc, getint, getch, getarray, putint, putch, putarray, starttime, stoptime). Also emits function prologues, epilogues, and the entry-block branch (llvmbodybeg).

nodes.label: Liveness Analysis
nodes.description: Iterative dataflow analysis over the control flow graph computing live-in and live-out temporary sets for each instruction node using the equations: in[n] = use[n] ∪ (out[n] − def[n]), out[n] = ∪_{s ∈ succ[n]} in[s]. Iterates until no in/out set changes. Stores results in the InOut table queried by interference graph construction.

nodes.label: Basic Block Graph Construction
nodes.description: Constructs a directed basic block graph (CFG at block granularity) from the LLVM IR block list produced by llvmgen. Registers each block as a graph node and adds edges according to successor labels. Then iteratively removes unreachable blocks (any block with no predecessors other than the entry block) to perform dead code elimination before SSA conversion.

nodes.label: Interference Graph Construction
nodes.description: Builds an undirected interference graph for register allocation. For each instruction, adds interference edges between every defined temporary and each live-out temporary, with special handling for move instructions to avoid spurious interference between the move's source and destination. Uses FG_def/FG_Out/ FG_use/FG_isMove from the flow graph and the Liveness-populated InOut table.

nodes.label: SSA Conversion
nodes.description: Converts the LLVM IR basic block graph (from Create_bg) into SSA form. Stages: topological sort of the CFG, block-level iterative liveness analysis, dominator set computation, immediate dominator and dominator-tree derivation, dominance frontier calculation, phi-function insertion at join points where a variable is live-in, and variable renaming via DFS over the dominator tree.

edges.label: llvmgen performs instruction selection on the Tiger IR basic blocks produced by the canon pipeline (C_linearize → C_basicBlocks → C_traceSchedule → C_basicBlocks); canon must produce a correct C_stmListList before llvmgen can translate it
Create_bg takes the AS_blockList assembled from llvmbodybeg + llvmbody outputs; bg needs valid LLVM IR blocks to construct the basic block graph
ssa() takes the G_nodeList returned by Create_bg as its sole CFG input; the basic block graph with dead code already removed must exist before SSA conversion begins
Liveness iterates over CFG nodes from FG_AssemFlowGraph and reads FG_use/FG_def/FG_In/FG_Out, all populated by flowgraph's UD table; the table must be initialized before liveness can iterate
Create_ig takes the Liveness-processed flow graph as input and reads FG_Out (live-out sets) populated by the Liveness iteration; interference edges cannot be derived without live-out information
